Transcrição Completa: 1 Power Of Cold Video

 

 

[00:00] [Music] You guys hear me talk about it all the time, the perfect cold video pitch. Usually, it's 7 to 12 minutes long. You're on your computer recording yourself on a desktop, laptop, nice background, you know, you got a well-lit face, they can hear you, you're confident, certain, and you're just demonstrating the results that you can drive for them with a specific process that you want to use uh to help drive them more leads and sales.

 

[00:28] Sometimes I teach this to my staff as far as my sales guys that come in. I ingrained this into the 11 salespeople I had at one time to rapidly duplicate the amount of communication we could get out in a day.

 

[00:40] When you're doing seven to 12 minute perfect cold video pitches, if you include the editing time and just the overall amount of time it takes to upload the videos and just record the videos, you're going to maybe get 10 to 50 a day depending on how hard you work and how many people that you have on your team that are doing them.

 

[00:58] You can obviously multiply yourself if you're getting 10 videos out per sales guy per day with the amount of sales people you have with the perfect cold video. However, an additional way to do that is to shrink the video size and change up the format for how you're shooting them.

 

[01:14] I actually give you guys this. This is something I've used in my agency. Uh I once again, I document and I record all my operational processes so I can give them out to my staff to have easy duplicated skills rapidly picked up by the rest of my team.

 

[01:28] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "60-Second Video Pitch Process"

 

NÚMEROS: 6 passos, 60 segundos, 10-50 vídeos/dia, 11 vendedores

 

VISUAL: Documento de processo estruturado, formato de treinamento operacional, sistema de 6 etapas destacado

 

 

[01:31] So, this is the 60-second video pitch. This is the process. Okay? So, you can download this below. It's right there on the video. It's also in the resources category along with 20 plus other things just like this.

 

[01:43] But here's the process, okay? Broke down into six steps. And let me explain the intention of the 60-second. This can be a this can be a warm follow-up. This can be another follow-up after your perfect cold video pitch. This could also be used in itself to create leads and generate some sales for you without having to do the 7 to 12 minute perfect cold video pitch all just on your phone with a vertical video.

 

[02:09] I had a mastermind here at the Miami compound downstairs in the community center. And everybody who came, there's roughly 10 maybe like 15 or so students and it was a three-day mastermind.

 

[02:21] Uh, it was great. I mean, everybody who came, they taught, they were taught this exact 60-second process. I had them go out here around the island and they were shooting videos for a good hour and a half, maybe two hours or so.

 

[02:33] And everybody I challenged to send at least a hundred of them. So, it only took two hours to send out about 100 videos because each video is a minute long. You just go from profile to profile to profile on Instagram or Facebook. You're just sending out these videos.

 

[02:48] So, long story short, there were a few different students who were here that had responses maybe an hour or so after they had started sending out their videos with people who they ended up closing as clients at later dates.

 

[03:01] So, long story short, this is effective and it works in real time. It's a it's a very short return as far as how long it takes usually to get people to communicate back. It's a unique approach as well, just a little shorter and a little less value driven because you don't have as much time.

 

[03:17] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Step 1: Create an Agreement"

 

NÚMEROS: Passo 1 de 6

 

VISUAL: Destaque do primeiro passo, ênfase em pesquisa prévia, elementos de validação inicial

 

 

[03:19] But the first thing you want to do, okay, here's here's the six-step process. You want to create an agreement. So, you want to quickly create something that you can have them recognize as something that shows that you've actually researched them and understand a little bit about them.

 

[03:35] So, let's say that I'm targeting Instagram fitness influencers. So, people who are working out all the time, posting pictures of their bodies, you know, they have uh whatever companies that they might have that they're repping or maybe they have their own company.

 

[03:49] Hopefully, they have your own their own company if you want to help them push product, of course. Or maybe they're an affiliate and they get a great commission on pushing whatever they're pushing, right?

 

[03:58] You just want to recognize something on their profile that's not going to be a generic statement. Okay? The 60-second perfect code video is not a generic video. You're still doing this unique to each person that you're sending it off to.

 

[04:11] So, to be clear, when I say you want to create an agreement, that means you want to have the person recognize that there's something you actually researched about them to showcase that you know about them and have done your research prior to this 60-second video and them giving it more attention.

 

[04:27] Check this out. You can go to their profile and quickly click around and read each caption. See what they're pushing. See what they're promoting. See how much engagement they're getting per video. See areas where there's room to grow and areas where there's currently clear weaknesses that can be called out where there can also be agreements created.

 

[04:45] Don't look to exploit or criticize at the beginning. Look to complement with something that they'll say, "Yep, that's exactly what I'm doing right now."

 

[04:53] So, for example, if a fitness influencer is currently plugging products, you see their posting schedule, meaning see when each post, how many times a day that they're getting content out there. So, you can understand how aggressively they're using social media and how much more help you can likely bring into their business depending on what you do.

 

[05:13] Let's say this is how my video starts. And I'm I'm not going to do the whole video at one time. I'm going break down each point for you. So, let's say my 60-second video starts with, "Hey, what's going on, Brandon? I'm reaching out right now. I see that on your profile you're posting three times a day. You're jacked, of course. And I'm noticing that you're plugging all your supplements a lot. You know, you're probably trying to sell more supplements, I imagine, from how many times a day you're posting these things right there.

 

[05:40] Okay, that's a statement that shows you understood something that was more advanced than just your plug-in product. It's I see you're posting three times a day. You see what I'm saying?

 

[05:50] If you go to somebody's website and you see all the different services that they're plugging, it might take you a total of 30 seconds to a minute depending on your internet speed and your ability to quickly scroll and identify what the person's doing business-wise.

 

[06:04] Think about it just in terms of business, okay? If you go to their profile, don't get overwhelmed by all the all the fancy visual aids that they're going to have on their profiles to sell people. Just understand how they're making money, okay?

 

[06:18] Understand how they're driving leads into their business. understand if they're trying to get DMs or if they have a professional website set up, maybe a funnel or a simple capture page, right? Understand the process and then call that process out in the first step, something that they can agree upon.

 

[06:34] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Step 2: Qualifying Agreement"

 

NÚMEROS: Passo 2 de 6, 3x/dia postagens

 

VISUAL: Transição para identificação de vulnerabilidades, análise de engajamento, destaque para oportunidades de melhoria

 

 

[06:36] Okay, the second step, qualifying agreement. Okay, you see on this piece of paper, if you download it, you're following along. Says, long story short, I want to identify the fact that you're producing incredible content, but you aren't getting shit for engagement.

 

[06:50] This is one thing that we loved inside of my business, okay? When we would agree with somebody, when we would create a statement that they could agree with, hey, we see that you're posting content three times a day. We love what you're doing. We see that you're promoting this kind of product. You likely want to sell more stuff.

 

[07:07] Qualifying agreement means we are going to then transition into what we need them to agree with that they might not be as happy about that we're bringing up or that they might not recognize as a positive, something that they can improve on, but without criticizing them in a negative way.

 

[07:23] So, the qualifying agreement as an example, just to reiterate that, hey, we see that you're posting content all the time, three times a day. That's great. We see your plug-in products. Looks like you got your own supplement company. I see on your website, too, looks like you have a coaching program, but on your page, it looks like you're getting shit for engagement.

 

[07:42] And what I mean by that, even though you have a lot of followers, you're getting a relatively small amount of people, it looks like you're just reaching the same audience every day, is my point. It looks like you might just be tapping the same people every day. it doesn't look like you're spending any money to distribute this content.

 

[07:58] So, what we do here is we bring up a vulnerability or a weakness without criticizing it and making it sound like it's negative. We just make it sound like there's something that they could do to make more money, drive more leads, but we want to make it clear here. This is something they should agree to as well.

 

[08:15] If you tell somebody they have shit engagement in a negative way and you just leave it at that, they're going to disagree with it and probably stop watching the video. If you say, "We're looking at your page and it looks like for everything you're doing, you're just not spending money to distribute your content to more people who don't know you yet. It looks like you just keep posting only towards the same people. You might not be putting a budget behind these things."

 

[08:38] And anyway, we got this cool new strategy. It was a real crisis way of identified and that's why we came up with it.

 

[08:44] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Step 3: Transition to Crisis State"

 

NÚMEROS: Passo 3 de 6, 6 arquétipos emocionais identificados por IA

 

VISUAL: Gráfico de altos e baixos emocionais, curva de storytelling, análise de todos os filmes por IA, pontos de pico emocional destacados

 

 

[08:46] Okay, transition to a crisis state. Now, here in the 60-second video, there's actually a study that was done by an artificial intelligence that watched every movie that has ever come out, and it was able to identify six key archetypes for every story that were commonalities between them.

 

[09:04] And it was emotional highs and emotional lows. Times where people rise in emotion and fall in emotion by listening and watching a story where somebody might be rising and falling in emotional peaks or emotional lows.

 

[09:17] So, long story short, you can use the power of tonality and emotion here in step number three because keep in mind in your 60-second video, you're kind of starting off in a neutral state. Okay? You're starting off creating an agreement. You're looking good and then you're transitioning to opening up an opportunity to expose a vulnerability somewhere that they could grow right now, something that they're going to say, you know what, that is an area that I can improve on.

 

[09:43] they're going to transition to a crisis state, which means you are going to identify with them through emotion that what you're saying is actually a crisis. So, my point is here's where you want to get a little more hype and you want to get a little more uppidity. Uh, and not necessarily like chaotic, but you want to use the power of emotion and tonality here.

 

[10:02] So, anyway, long story short, when you transition to the crisis state, and I'm breaking all this down for you. I'll have a smooth 60-second version of this at the end for you. We're going to get into step three now. So, I'll start with step one. I'll get here. Okay.

 

[10:16] Hey, what's going on, Brandon? I'm looking at your profile right now. It looks awesome. All your content's great. Looks like you're posting three times a day. I noticed though that you're getting shit engagement. It doesn't look like that you're spending money to distribute your content towards people who don't know you yet. It looks like you're only using the people who are currently seeing your posts as the people who are engaging with that.

 

[10:38] So, I'm not saying it's shitty, but I'm saying that there's more opportunity to grow. This is a real crisis, okay? There's people right now in your industry that are just posting again and again, and everybody's doing it. It's across every industry.

 

[10:51] People right now don't understand that you have to spend a little bit of money to distribute content. We're all used to the old Instagram algorithm and thinking that people are sharing our content organically. It's not happening anymore. Mashable's talking about it. Ink.com's talking about Forbes is talking about it.

 

[11:07] Now, what I've done there is I've identified a crisis. Okay. I've leveraged some different sources of information where I could send that person news articles if I wanted to on it afterwards if they asked about stories talking about pages getting less reach. uh the the amount of engagement going down and dwindling.

 

[11:25] You want supporting evidence here. I want to be clear, there's always supporting evidence for both sides of any argument, especially nowadays on the internet. So, you can find something that helps you with your crisis.

 

[11:36] Maybe you do video explainers for what you sell. You could say, "Hey, what's going on, Brandon? I'm looking at your page right now. All your content's awesome. You're posting three times a day. I noticed that you're taking photos and videos all the time, which is great, but you don't have any animated videos on your pages. You're not using animations yet to help explain what you do, who you are, and how you do it.

 

[11:58] And this is a real crisis going on, not just with you, but with everybody. Everybody right now on the internet is not understanding that if you cartoonify yourself into little explainer videos, that the engagement on average is about 3 to 4x higher than what it is if you're just doing regular videos.

 

[12:16] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Step 4: Ask the Why, How, and Who"

 

NÚMEROS: Passo 4 de 6, engajamento 3-4x maior com vídeos animados

 

VISUAL: Técnica de transferência de responsabilidade, psicologia de vendas avançada, remoção de culpa do prospect

 

 

[12:18] Now, here's what you do for step four. Step four is ask the why, the how, and the who. So, you take the responsibility of accepting the problem off of the person that you're making the video for.

 

[12:30] So, you want to make sure that the person doesn't feel guilt, shame, or like they're doing something wrong. This is just to further fortify that it's not their fault, and that you want to let them know that somebody else should be responsible for it.

 

[12:42] There's a little bit of subconscious play going on here that is a little more advanced for everybody that wants to know. What you're doing here is you're making sure that they don't feel responsibility for the problem like they can solve it. you're asking and taking the responsibility of that problem being able to get solved, acting as if it's supposed to be handled by somebody else in their business already.

 

[13:04] And they'll assume at that point that if they don't have somebody to do it that they need somebody else to do it, and they'll have a lower probability of inheriting the responsibility themselves.

 

[13:14] But without without going too far down the the psychology rabbit hole, I'm just going to act as though I I'm doing the video again. And now I'm going to ask the why, the how, and the who. and I'm going to transition into phase four to make sure I take that pressure off them. Keep in mind it's all in 60 seconds. You ready?

 

[13:31] Okay, here we go. Agreement to start. Hey, what's going on, Brandon? I'm looking at your page right now. Seeing you're posting awesome content, posting three times a day. That's great. But what I'm noticing is that you're not distributing your content right now. It looks like you're getting shit engagement.

 

[13:47] And what I mean is it just looks like you're posting without paying many money to get in front of cold audiences who don't know you yet. That way, you can expand the amount of people who don't know you and build your audience up even more, get new customers, get more leads.

 

[14:01] This is a true crisis right now. Not just for you, but it's for everybody. Everybody in this industry is not distributing their content. We're all conditioned based on the old algorithms that our content gets distributed chronologically and that there's all these share algorithms. It's just not happening anymore. Look, Mashable's talking about it, Inc.'s talking about it, Forbes's talking about it. Reach is lowered and it's a true crisis.

 

[14:24] Once again, I understand though, this is not your fault. You likely don't even know why this is happening because there's so much to know about it. You know, I'm wondering though, who in your business is doing this? And how are they going to plan to get around this to get you more eyeballs and to make you more money?

 

[14:41] See what I did? I'm taking the responsibility once again off of them and I'm putting it into their team or somebody that should already be helping them. Use the assumptive state here to assume that they should already be doing it.

 

[14:54] Don't act like they need to be doing it like it's something new, cool, and fancy. It's very rare we're going to sell a service, especially in the digital marketing world, that's new and cool and fancy. It's probably something that they already are aware of at least.

 

[15:07] And you want to assume, you want to project assumption onto them that they should already be doing it to make them even more certain that it's something that they're missing out on. Right? Give them a little FOMO, fear of missing out. Okay?

 

[15:20] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Step 5: The What - Solution Presentation"

 

NÚMEROS: Passo 5 de 6

 

VISUAL: Apresentação da solução, estrutura de equipe, plano de ação, posicionamento como especialista

 

 

[15:22] Now, over here is then the what. So, what are you going to do to solve this crisis? Okay? What's the plan? How are you going to handle this? Right? Do you have a team? Do you have a staff? Who's going to help? Okay? So, that's what you're bringing into the video right here.

 

[15:38] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Step 6: Call to Action - Two Options That Benefit Me"

 

NÚMEROS: Passo 6 de 6, 2 opções (não sim/não)

 

VISUAL: Estratégia de fechamento com dupla escolha, eliminação de resposta sim/não, opções: manhã ou tarde, falar com equipe ou direto

 

 

[15:40] And step six is call to action. Two options that benefit me. So, what that means is as the person who's going to be getting the video, we want to make sure we give them two clear routes here. We don't want to give them one option like, "Hey, can we talk on the phone?" Because then that option that they have is simple yes or no. That doesn't play into your favor.

 

[15:59] Two options like, "Do you want me to help your team solve this? Do you want me to talk with them? Or should I talk with you about this? And when can we talk?" You see what I'm saying? That helps with having two decisions that are not yes and no. So, they'll have a higher probability of making one of those decisions versus you having a higher probability of getting told no.

 

[16:19] So anyway, long story short, uh dude, we have to talk. I'm going to make I'm going to make myself available for a phone call. Uh does the morning or afternoon work? You know, can I talk to your team or should I talk to you about this first? Are are you the person who's going to make the decision on this?

 

[16:35] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Complete 60-Second Video Example"

 

NÚMEROS: 60 segundos totais, exemplo completo integrado

 

VISUAL: Demonstração completa do pitch, influencer fitness como exemplo, integração de todos os 6 passos em sequência

 

 

[16:37] So anyway, here's the whole video through and through. Okay, you got your phone. We're going to act as though we got our fitness influencer once again. Here we go.

 

[16:46] Hey, what's going on, Brandon? My name's Jeremy. I'm reaching out to you. I see you're posting content all the time. It looks great. I see you're posting three times a day. However, what I noticed is it doesn't look like you're getting as many eyeballs in your content as you should. You kind of got shit engagement. and it looks like you're posting relying upon the algorithm to distribute your content instead of putting a little money behind it.

 

[17:11] And this is a true crisis. There's so many people in this industry just like you that are constantly right now not putting a little bit of money, five bucks a day even to distribute their content out there and they're going down into obscurity.

 

[17:24] We need to have omnipresence around the people who know us because it drives more leads and sales into our business. I want to ask though, you know, why isn't your team currently solving this? you know, who who should be responsible for this inside of your business right now, and why aren't they currently handling it?

 

[17:41] You know, what are you going to be doing to solve this problem? You know, I'd love to help. Uh, I want to make sure that I make myself available to you or your team. I just love to know who I can talk to and when. Can we talk in the morning or should we talk in the afternoon?

 

[17:56] Okay, cool. Now, that's a 60-second video pitch. You can slang those all day and as soon as you keep doing them, you'll get better and better and better and better.

 

[18:05] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Distribution Strategy & Tools"

 

NÚMEROS: 100 vídeos em 2 horas, $5/dia investimento mínimo

 

VISUAL: Iconosquare.com como ferramenta, estratégia Instagram/Facebook, filtros de threshold, processo de identificação de perfis ideais

 

 

[18:07] The ways to distribute these, really simple. Just go on Instagram, find some ideal clients that you want to work with. You can use tools like iconosquare.com to look up people in different thresholds and just get a long lead list of Instagram profiles that you can go and tap.

 

[18:23] And long story short, you just want to leverage that button where when you follow people or you have that drop down that tells you the next four or five people that are similar to them and just rotate between people and identify who's good for your business and who's not based on the perfect client traits and the do not do client trades.

 

[18:40] Okay, cool. See you in the next video. May she download this.

 

 

📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO

 

NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:

7-12 minutos: duração do pitch de vídeo frio perfeito tradicional

60 segundos: duração do novo formato de pitch otimizado

10-50 vídeos/dia: produção possível com formato tradicional

100 vídeos em 2 horas: produção com formato de 60 segundos

11 vendedores: equipe treinada pelo instrutor

10-15 estudantes: participantes do mastermind de 3 dias em Miami

1 hora: tempo médio para primeiras respostas

$5/dia: investimento mínimo sugerido para distribuição de conteúdo

3x/dia: frequência de postagem típica de influencers

3-4x: aumento de engajamento com vídeos animados vs. regulares

6 passos: estrutura do processo de pitch de 60 segundos

6 arquétipos emocionais: identificados por IA analisando todos os filmes

20+ recursos: disponíveis na categoria de recursos

 

ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:

 

Pitch de Vídeo Frio Tradicional (7-12 min):

Gravação em desktop/laptop

Background profissional e iluminação adequada

Demonstração de resultados e processos específicos

Inclui tempo de edição e upload

 

Pitch de Vídeo de 60 Segundos (6 Passos):

Passo 1 - Criar Acordo: Demonstrar pesquisa personalizada, não ser genérico

Passo 2 - Acordo Qualificador: Identificar vulnerabilidades sem criticar negativamente

Passo 3 - Estado de Crise: Usar tonalidade e emoção, apresentar evidências (Mashable, Inc., Forbes)

Passo 4 - Perguntar Por Quê/Como/Quem: Transferir responsabilidade do problema para equipe/terceiros

Passo 5 - O Quê: Apresentar solução e plano de ação

Passo 6 - Call to Action: Oferecer duas opções (não sim/não) - manhã ou tarde, falar com equipe ou direto

 

Estratégia de Distribuição:

Instagram/Facebook como plataformas principais

Vídeo vertical no celular

Perfil para perfil, DM direto

Uso de ferramentas: iconosquare.com para pesquisa de perfis

Identificação de perfis similares através de sugestões

Filtros de threshold para segmentação

 

Psicologia de Vendas Aplicada:

Arquétipos emocionais (altos e baixos)

Remoção de culpa do prospect

Projeção de responsabilidade para equipe existente

Assumir que já deveriam estar fazendo

Criar FOMO (Fear of Missing Out)

Evitar formato sim/não em CTAs

 

ESTRUTURA:

 

Formato Tradicional: Desktop/laptop, 7-12 minutos, alta produção

Formato Otimizado: Mobile, vertical, 60 segundos, baixa produção

Aplicações: Cold outreach, warm follow-up, follow-up pós-pitch tradicional

Escalabilidade: Multiplicação através de equipe de vendas

Validação: Testado em mastermind de 3 dias com resultados em 1 hora

Documentação: Processos operacionais documentados para replicação

Target Principal: Influencers fitness, empreendedores digitais, donos de negócios com presença social

 

DADOS FINANCEIROS:

Investimento mínimo: $5/dia para distribuição de conteúdo

ROI: Fechamentos de clientes em menos de 1 hora após envio

Custo de produção: Zero (apenas celular)

Escalabilidade: 100 vídeos/2 horas vs. 10-50 vídeos/dia (formato tradicional)

 

FONTES DE AUTORIDADE CITADAS:

Mashable

Inc.com

Forbes

Estudo de IA sobre arquétipos emocionais em filmes

 

FERRAMENTAS MENCIONADAS:

Iconosquare.com (pesquisa de perfis e thresholds)

Instagram/Facebook (distribuição)

Celular (gravação vertical)

 

DURAÇÃO: 18:40 FORMATO: Vídeo educacional/treinamento de vendas com demonstração prática
